title: Shaliz'Na

{{Quote|You've got to look for beauty in it all.|Tyric, Shaliz'Na musician|<ref name="POL" />}}
The '''Shaliz'Na''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shar'Ack]]. The species had large [[Limb/Legends|wings]], some with wingspans of 12 [[Foot/Legends|foot]], and their bodies were covered in a fine down. Shaliz'na placed a premium on art of all forms, from [[Music/Legends|music]] to sculptures to paintings. As a species, they were addicted to [[Alcohol/Legends|alcohol]], and when conquered by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], liquor was strictly controlled on the world. Some members of the species left their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] in an attempt to break their addiction; the [[Musician/Legends|musician]] [[Tyric (Shaliz'Na)|Tyric]] was one such Shaliz'Na, eventually joining the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] in its struggle to overthrow the Empire.

==Biology and appearance==
The Shaliz'Na were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="POL" /> with large, feathered [[Limb/Legends|wings]].<ref name="POL 63" /> Their bodies were muscular and covered in light down. Their facial features were [[Human/Legends|Human]]-like, with head [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that was black in at least some cases.<ref name="POL" /> Some Shaliz'Na grew this down to their shoulders, held back by a patterned headband.<ref name="POL 63" /> They had talon-like fingernails, which were dexterous enough to play a [[Synthesizer/Legends|synthesizer]]. The Shaliz'Na [[Tyric (Shaliz'Na)|Tyric]] was 2.14 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall with a wing span of 12 [[Foot/Legends|foot]].<ref name="POL" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|Tyric eventually overcame his alcoholism; his music sweetened as a result.|[[Scarlet Bloodhawk]]|<ref name="POL" />}}
The Shaliz'Na were a species of artisans, holding artworks of all forms in the deepest regard. Some, like Tyric, were prepared to take significant personal risks to protect that which they considered art. When offworld, Tyric would even accept sculptures, paintings, and other ornamental objects in lieu of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. As opposed to the [[Laser/Legends|laser]] technology common across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the Shaliz'Na developed chemically-propelled weapons, including [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrowers]] and mini-[[submachinegun]]s.<ref name="POL" /> Shaliz'Na wore clothes such as open [[shirt]]s, with short sleeves decorated with hanging tassels, pants, [[Belt/Legends|belts]] and patterned headbands.<ref name="POL 63" />

The Shaliz'Na were addicted to [[Alcohol/Legends|alcohol]], and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] the [[Planet/Legends|world]] and their exclusive control over all liquor manufactured and imported to the planet helped maintain their control over the natives.<ref name="POL" />

==History==
{{Quote|Maizie's was really hopping with some bird-man named Tyric playing the synthesizer, and the light displays were going full blast.|Scarlet Bloodhawk|<ref name="POL" />}}
The Shaliz'Na evolved on the planet Shar'Ack,<ref name="POL" /> located in the [[Shar'Ack system/Legends|Shar'Ack system]] of the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]].<ref name="Atlas">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The species became addicted to alcohol. When their forested [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was conquered by the Galactic Empire and the Shaliz'Na enslaved, alcohol became strictly controlled. That led to some members of the species leaving their world to recover from alcoholism. One such traveler was the musician Tyric, who devoted himself to traveling and [[Music/Legends|music]] to try and break his addiction. He found support from [[Kelly Vermillion]], a half-Human/half-[[Orlak]], and together with two others, they formed the crew of the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Menagerie]]''. The group joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], making its mark on the resistance movement. In that supportive environment, Tyric eventually recovered from his alcoholism.<ref name="POL" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Shaliz'Na were created by [[Joe Littrell]] for the article ''[[The New Rogues Gallery: Unsung Heroes of the Rebellion]]'', published in ''[[Polyhedron 59]]''. An illustration was commissioned from [[Andrew Ellis]] to accompany the article, but due to space constraints, was instead published as part of the article ''[[The Living Galaxy: Flawed Gems Shine The Brightest]]'' in ''[[Polyhedron 63]]''. At that time, the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="POL" /> rendering the article non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> In [[2009]], the species' home star system was referenced in the appendix of ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''; however, according to [[Jason Fry]], "its inclusion shouldn't be taken as a statement of canonicity for anything except the geography."<ref name="Fry">{{JCFCite|url=posts/29648348|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2009-01-04|time=7:52 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200714135715/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-essential-atlas-and-galactic-cartography-official-discussion.21938930/page-72}}</ref>
